safest_plan.entry_basis: No entry given — all three SPY mentions are educational slides (sector-rotation primer, passive/active framework, benchmark-return chart) with no price levels.
safest_plan.stop_basis: No structure-based stop — the sources carry no SPY levels, only teaching content.

stance_by_source.summary: Educational 'Big 6' sector-rotation slide naming the SPDR sector ETFs and how money rotates between cyclical and defensive sectors — no SPY entry or level.
Passive-vs-active strategy framework arguing for a hybrid approach (stay invested in the index core, time only the satellite) — general method, not an SPY trade.
Performance-benchmark chart: over 2020–2025 the S&P 500 returned about +70% (~10–12% a year); the lesson is to beat the passive index, not a call to buy at a level.

plain_english: Everything tagged to SPY here is teaching material from a Zero live session, not a trade idea: one slide explains how money rotates between stock-market sectors, one compares buy-and-hold versus active trading, and one shows that the S&P 500 grew about 70% over the last five years as a yardstick to measure your own returns against. None of them give a price to buy at, a target, or a level that would say the idea is wrong. There is nothing to act on — treat it as background education and stand aside.
